description: x64dbg Command Database - Structured command definitions for MCP integration

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Path to the executable file to debug.

init.result: This command does not set any result variables.

run.description: Resume execution of the debugged process.
run.result: This command does not set any result variables.

stop.result: This command does not set any result variables.

StepInto.result: This command does not set any result variables.

StepOver.result: This command does not set any result variables.

StepOut.description: Step out of the current function (run until return).
StepOut.result: This command does not set any result variables.

attach.description: Attach the debugger to a running process.
attach.result: This command does not set any result variables.

detach.description: Detach the debugger from the debugged process.
detach.result: This command does not set any result variables.

SetBPX.description: Set a software breakpoint at the specified address.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to set the breakpoint at.

SetBPX.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the breakpoint to delete.

DeleteBPX.result: This command does not set any result variables.

SetHardwareBreakpoint.description: Set a hardware breakpoint at the specified address.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to set the breakpoint at.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Size of the breakpoint (1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes). Default is 1.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Type: 'r' for read, 'w' for write, 'x' for execute (default).

SetHardwareBreakpoint.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the hardware breakpoint to delete.

DeleteHardwareBreakpoint.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Type: 'r' for read, 'w' for write, 'x' for execute.

SetMemoryBPX.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Name of the DLL to break on (can use wildcards).

LibrarianSetBreakpoint.result: This command does not set any result variables.

SetExceptionBPX.result: This command does not set any result variables.

alloc.description: Allocate memory in the debuggee process.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Size of memory to allocate (in bytes).

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Preferred base address. Default is 0 (any address).

alloc.result: The $result variable is set to the allocated memory address.

free.description: Free memory in the debuggee process.
free.result: This command does not set any result variables.

Fill.description: Fill memory with a byte pattern.
Fill.result: This command does not set any result variables.

memcpy.description: Copy memory from one location to another.
memcpy.result: This command does not set any result variables.

getpagerights.result: The $result variable is set to the page rights string.

setpagerights.result: This command does not set any result variables.

savedata.description: Save memory region to a file.
savedata.result: This command does not set any result variables.

minidump.description: Create a minidump of the debugged process.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: File path to save the minidump.

minidump.result: This command does not set any result variables.

find.description: Find a pattern in a memory page.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Size to search in. Default is memory region size.

find.result: The $result variable is set to the address of the first match, or 0 if not found.

findall.description: Find all occurrences of a pattern in a memory page.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Size to search in. Default is memory region size.

findall.result: $result is set to the number of occurrences. Results appear in References view.

findallmem.description: Find all occurrences of a pattern in the entire memory map.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Size to search in. Default is entire memory (-1).

findallmem.result: $result is set to the number of occurrences.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Address to start searching. Default is CIP.

findasm.result: $result is set to the number of references found.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Address to start searching. Default is CIP.

reffind.result: $result is set to the number of references found.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to start searching. Default is CIP.

refstr.result: $result is set to the number of string references found.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to start searching. Default is EIP/RIP.

modcallfind.result: $result is set to the number of inter-modular calls found.

analyse.description: Perform function analysis on the current module.
analyse.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Base address of the function to analyze.

analrecur.result: This command does not set any result variables.

analxrefs.description: Perform cross-reference analysis in the current module.
analxrefs.result: This command does not set any result variables.

symdownload.description: Download symbols from a Symbol Store.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Module name. If not specified, downloads for all modules.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Symbol Store URL. If not specified, uses default.

symdownload.result: This command does not set any result variables.

exhandlers.description: Print all exception handlers (SEH, VEH, VCH, UnhandledExceptionFilter).
exhandlers.result: This command does not set any result variables.

createthread.description: Create a new thread in the debugged process.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Parameter to pass to the thread.

createthread.result: This command does not set any result variables.

switchthread.result: This command does not set any result variables.

suspendthread.result: This command does not set any result variables.

resumethread.result: This command does not set any result variables.

killthread.result: This command does not set any result variables.

TraceIntoConditional.description: Trace into until condition is met.
TraceIntoConditional.result: This command does not set any result variables.

TraceOverConditional.description: Trace over until condition is met.
TraceOverConditional.result: This command does not set any result variables.

tibt.result: This command does not set any result variables.

tobt.result: This command does not set any result variables.

user_database.description: User database operations (comments, labels, bookmarks, functions)

commentset.description: Set a comment at an address.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to set the comment at.

commentset.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the comment to delete.

commentdel.result: This command does not set any result variables.

labelset.description: Set a label at an address.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to set the label at.

labelset.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the label to delete.

labeldel.result: This command does not set any result variables.

bookmarkset.description: Set a bookmark at an address.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to set the bookmark at.

bookmarkset.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the bookmark to delete.

bookmarkdel.result: This command does not set any result variables.

functionadd.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address inside the function range to delete.

functiondel.result: This command does not set any result variables.

var.result: This command does not set any result variables.

vardel.result: This command does not set any result variables.

varlist.result: This command does not set any result variables.

general_purpose.description: General purpose operations (mov, add, sub, etc.)

mov.description: Set a variable or register value.
mov.result: This command does not set any result variables.

add.result: This command does not set any result variables.

sub.result: This command does not set any result variables.

xor.result: This command does not set any result variables.

cmp.result: Sets $_EZ_FLAG (1 if equal) and $_BS_FLAG (1 if arg1 > arg2).

push.description: Push a value on the stack.
push.result: This command does not set any result variables.

pop.description: Pop a value from the stack.
pop.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Path of a directory to change to.

chd.result: This command does not set any result variables.

loadlib.description: Load a DLL into debugged program memory.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Name/path of the module to load.

loadlib.result: The $result variable will be set to the address of the loaded library.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to place the assembled instruction.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Fill remainder with NOPs if specified.

asm.result: $result will be set to the assembled instruction size. 0 on failure.

gpa.description: Get the address of an export inside a DLL.
gpa.result: The $result variable is set to the export address. 0 if not found.

HideDebugger.description: Hide the debugger from simple detection methods. Modifies PEB.
HideDebugger.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to disassemble at. Default is CIP.

disasm.result: This command does not set any result variables.

dump.result: This command does not set any result variables.

graph.description: Graph the control flow of a function.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address of the function. Default is EIP/RIP.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Options: 'force' to reanalyze, 'silent' to suppress messages.

graph.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Title of the new reference view.

refinit.result: This command does not set any result variables.

refadd.description: Add an entry to the reference view.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Address to put in the reference view.

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Text to put after the address.

refadd.result: This command does not set any result variables.

log.result: This command does not set any result variables.

msg.result: This command does not set any result variables.

scriptload.result: This command does not set any result variables.

scriptexec.description: Load and execute a script file in a single operation.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Script file path to load and execute.

scriptexec.result: This command does not set any result variables.

EnablePrivilege.result: This command does not set any result variables.

DisablePrivilege.result: This command does not set any result variables.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Handle value in the context of the debuggee.

handleclose.result: This command does not set any result variables.
